mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate at 5.25% keeps discount rates elevated, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-rate periods show statistically indistinguishable mean returns from low-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 6-month trailing low, it signals labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessionary equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 6-month minimum
failure_condition: In periods where the 0.3pp UNRATE trigger fires, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a high federal funds rate signals demand resilience despite tight policy, which the market interprets as confirmation of a soft landing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; this effect requires both conditions jointly since either alone does not disambiguate soft-landing from overheating scenarios.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 20d returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when housing starts (HOUST) are declining from cyclical highs, the housing sector acts as a leading indicator of broader demand contraction, and equities underperform over the following quarter; this relationship is only powerful in late-cycle regimes when rates are elevated, since housing is highly rate-sensitive.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: During HOUST decline episodes coinciding with FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, falsifying the housing-led demand contraction thesis.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic supply capacity and corporate output, which is incorporated quickly into equity prices, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO change and forward 5d returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) decelerates sharply, falling below a 50,000 monthly gain threshold, it signals an inflection toward labor market weakness that leads institutional investors to reduce equity risk exposure over the subsequent month, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 20d returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or the effect size is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation remains elevated while the federal funds rate is simultaneously high, the market perceives a policy stalemate where the Fed cannot ease, jointly amplifying near-term volatility and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week; neither high inflation nor high rates alone captures this stalemate dynamic.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 5d returns statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of decelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with elevated interest rates, equities are more sensitive to negative growth surprises, so weak GDP prints amplify downside risk over the following quarter; in expansionary regimes with low rates, the same GDP weakness has muted equity impact.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: In periods of sub-1.5% GDP growth coinciding with FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, rejecting the growth-sensitivity thesis.
